message:
feat(lexical): scale cass Tantivy writer to available CPU cores with bulk-load merge policy

The cass-compatible Tantivy writer previously used a single indexing thread
with a 50MB heap, which severely underutilized multi-core machines during
large lexical rebuilds of multi-million-message corpora.

Writer scaling:
- Add CassWriterConfig struct and cass_writer_config() which scales writer
  threads up to CASS_MAX_WRITER_THREADS (4) based on available_parallelism()
- Scale heap budget with thread count (128MB per thread, 256MB minimum)
  so each indexing thread has sufficient in-memory runway before Tantivy
  flushes segments
- Add tracing for configured thread count and heap size at writer open

Bulk-load merge policy:
- Add configure_bulk_load_merge_policy() which sets a LogMergePolicy with
  CASS_BULK_LOAD_MIN_SEGMENTS_PER_MERGE (32) to delay background merges
  during bulk rebuilds — avoiding near-continuous compaction while the
  canonical database is still streaming documents
- Callers can invoke this during rebuild-from-scratch operations and
  revert to default merge policy afterward

Test coverage:
- cass_writer_config_scales_with_parallelism: verifies thread clamping at 1,
  2, and 8 cores with correct heap budget calculations

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
